retry_reason: kref_put is a release operation; retrying would cause double-put and use-after-free. The NULL check makes it safe to call on NULL, but success is always determinable.
contract_summary: This is a put wrapper that releases a reference on an `aa_label`. It decrements the reference count via `kref_put` and invokes `aa_label_kref` when the last reference is dropped. The caller must ensure that `l` is a valid pointer to a label whose reference count is being decremented; calling it on a NULL pointer is safe (no-op). After calling this function, the caller must not access the label object unless it holds another reference (i.e., the caller's reference is consumed). This is the release side of the label's reference-count lifecycle.
